gui_label_input: Paste or edit an ASCII tree (supports `tree` command output):
gui_msg_no_input_content: Please input or import an ASCII tree first.
gui_msg_generate_confirm:
This will create empty folders and empty files in the following directory ({count} node(s)):

{path}

Please verify the preview on the right. When "Abort on path conflict" is unchecked, items with the same name may be overwritten.

Continue?
gui_msg_import_parse:
Parse the imported content now?
(You may adjust the options below first; choose "No" to parse manually with "Parse tree".)
gui_msg_close_confirm: The input box contains unsaved content. Exit anyway?
gui_msg_view_all_warnings_hint: (Click "All warnings" or double-click the status bar to see the full list.)
gui_msg_about:
Treeing
Version {version}

[Overview]
Turns ASCII directory trees (e.g. `tree` command output) into real empty directories and empty files.
Supports Unicode box-drawing, pipe style, and Windows `tree` formats; provides both a GUI and a CLI.

[Main features]
• Parse and preview directory tree structures
• Generate empty directories and files in one click
• Optional strict directory inference, abort-on-conflict, nested path names, and more
• CLI supports JSON output, dry-run, and scripting

[Usage tips]
• GUI: Ctrl+Enter to parse, Ctrl+G to generate
  Launch: {gui_python} (source)
          {gui_exe_unix} (Linux / macOS executable) / {gui_exe_win} (Windows executable)
• CLI:
  Source: {cli_python} --help
  Executable: {cli_exe_unix} --help (Linux / macOS) / {cli_exe_win} --help (Windows)
• See the user guide for details.

[Privacy]
This is a fully local, offline tool. It does not connect to the network, upload anything, or collect personal data.
It only stores UI preferences (font size, last output directory, import encoding, and the encoding drop-down presets) under ~/.treeing/settings.json in your home directory, to restore them on next launch, and they never leave your machine.
You may delete that file at any time; the app will fall back to defaults.
To report issues, please use the Issues page on the project homepage.

[AI disclosure]
Parts of this source code were written with assistance from AI coding tools.

[Licence & credits]
Copyright (c) 2026 MING
MIT Licence — see the project LICENSE file

[Project homepage]
https://github.com/xiao-ming-b65536/treeing
gui_err_tcl_missing:
Error: could not initialise the GUI; the Tcl/Tk runtime in the current environment is incomplete.
Please install a full Tcl/Tk, or use the CLI:
  {cli_python} --help          # source
  {cli_exe_unix} --help        # Linux / macOS executable
  {cli_exe_win} --help         # Windows executable
gui_file_dialog_title_open: Choose a text file to import
gui_tooltip_input:
Paste or write a "directory tree diagram" here, such as the output of the `tree` command or an ASCII tree with ├──.
Not plain prose, logs, or source code.
Notes:
1. Content pasted directly is read as UTF-8 and is not affected by the "File import encoding" option on the right.
2. Lines starting with # or // are treated as comments and ignored.
gui_tooltip_preview:
Click "Parse tree" first; the recognised folders and files will appear here (names cleaned per generation rules).
Folders end with /.
Grey italic items are virtual nodes (shown as [virtual] …) and will not be written to disk.
Notes:
1. Preview is read-only.
2. If a name has been cleaned, it shows "disk name (original name)".
gui_tooltip_indent_unit:
How many characters each level is indented.
Usually leave blank and let the program auto-detect (typically 2 or 4).
For hand-written trees with a fixed 2-space indent per level, enter 2; for 4 characters per level, enter 4.
Notes:
1. Only integers greater than 0 are accepted.
2. An invalid or non-numeric value is treated as "auto".
3. A wrong value may break parent-child relationships—check the preview on the right.
gui_tooltip_encoding:
Text encoding used when opening "Import text file" or dropping a file.
Pick a common one from the dropdown, or type another encoding name (e.g. cp1252).
The dropdown presets can be edited in import_encodings under ~/.treeing/settings.json.
Notes:
1. Does not affect content pasted directly into the input box (paste is always UTF-8).
2. A wrong encoding name causes import to fail.
gui_tooltip_import:
Choose a "directory tree diagram" text file (e.g. tree.txt, asciitree.txt).
The content should be a folder hierarchy diagram, not notes, prose, or regular source files.
After a successful import you will be asked whether to parse immediately; you may adjust the options below first.
Notes:
1. Encoding is decided by the "File import encoding" dropdown (manual entry allowed).
2. Import replaces the current input box content.
3. A wrong encoding raises an error.
4. Shortcut: Ctrl+O.
gui_tooltip_clear:
Clear the input box on the left and the preview on the right.
Notes:
1. No second confirmation.
2. Cannot be undone—back up your text first.
3. Shortcut: Ctrl+L.
gui_tooltip_parse:
Analyse the directory tree structure based on the current input and the checkboxes below.
Results appear in the preview on the right; problems raise a dialogue.
Notes:
1. Empty input shows a notice.
2. If no line can be recognised, a notice is shown.
3. When there are many warnings, the dialogue shows up to 8; click "All warnings" for the full list.
4. Shortcut: Ctrl+Enter.
gui_tooltip_generate:
Create folders and empty files in the directory you choose, based on the tree diagram on the left.
The left side must be a tree or ASCII tree diagram, not plain prose or notes.
Click "Parse tree" first and review the preview before clicking this button.
Notes:
1. Only empty folders and empty files are created; the diagram text is not copied into files.
2. After choosing the target folder you will be asked to confirm; Cancel aborts without writing.
3. When "Abort on path conflict" is unchecked, a later item with the same name overwrites an earlier one.
4. Shortcut: Ctrl+G; the last target directory is remembered.
gui_tooltip_exit:
Close the program.
You can also click the × at the top-right of the window.
Notes:
1. Input box content is not saved automatically; if there is content, you will be asked to confirm.
gui_tooltip_about: Show the program version, feature overview, and licence information.
gui_tooltip_allow_nested:
Usually leave this unchecked.
Check it only when a single line contains a slash path like src/main/app.py and you want it created as real folder levels.
Notes:
1. Use only with content you trust.
2. Paths containing .. or starting with a drive letter like C:\ or D:\ are rejected.
3. When unchecked, the whole line is treated as one filename (slashes become underscores, etc.).
gui_tooltip_fail_on_conflict:
When checked, any duplicate name, or a name used as both a file and a folder, immediately aborts and rolls back what was created this run.
Notes:
1. Only affects "Generate directories/files".
2. Pre-existing content in the target folder is not deleted.
3. On a failure such as missing write permission, it also tries to roll back what was just created.
gui_tooltip_strict_dirs:
Usually leave this unchecked (the default is fine).
Decides how lines that do not clearly indicate whether they are a file or a folder are interpreted.
Checked: names without an extension like .py or .txt default to folders (e.g. SubDir).
Unchecked: names with uppercase letters may be treated as files instead of folders.
Notes:
1. Only affects lines with branch symbols like ├──.
2. Lines ending with / are always folders in both modes.
3. Anything with children below it is always a folder.
4. Common filenames like Makefile and README are always files.
gui_tooltip_no_fix:
When checked, irregular indentation is no longer auto-repaired.
Enable only when the input is already well-aligned and you want to parse it verbatim.
Notes:
1. With messy indentation you may get more warnings or grey temporary levels.
2. Can be combined with "Indent unit".
gui_tooltip_toggle_tree:
Toggle between fully expanded and fully collapsed.
▼ fully expanded (click to collapse); ▶ fully collapsed (click to expand).
Notes:
1. Affects the preview area only; does not modify the input.
2. After manually expanding/collapsing individual nodes, the icon may not match the actual state exactly.
gui_tooltip_view_warnings:
View all current parse/generation warnings in a dialogue.
The button is disabled when there are no warnings; you can also double-click the status bar at the bottom.
Notes:
1. Shows only the warnings from the most recent parse/generation in this session.
gui_tooltip_font_size:
Adjust the monospace font size (8–24) of the input box on the left.
The setting is saved to ~/.treeing/settings.json in your user directory.
Notes:
1. Affects only the input box font, not the preview area.
cli_help_desc:
Treeing — ASCII tree → directory/file generator (CLI)
Turns `tree` command output or hand-written ASCII trees into real empty directories and empty files.
cli_help_epilog:
Usage (<args> shown below):
  {cli_python} <args>          # source
  {cli_exe_unix} <args>        # Linux / macOS executable
  {cli_exe_win} <args>         # Windows executable

Example <args>:
  -i tree.txt -o ./out --dry-run
  -i tree.txt -o ./out --confirm
  -i tree.txt -o ./out --yes --fail-on-conflict
  help confirm       # write confirmation
  --help-full        # full help
  --about            # about

More: help (topic list) | see the user guide
cli_help_full_flag: Show full help (detailed description of each option)
cli_help_topic_automation:
(Example) Recommended CI / agent combos:
  --dry-run --json --quiet --warn-exit-code --fail-on-conflict
  --yes --json --quiet --fail-on-conflict
  --warnings-file /path/to/log
Notes:
1. Always pass -o explicitly.
2. Dry-run first, then --yes for the real run.
3. Exit codes: 0 success, 1 failure, 2 warnings (needs --warn-exit-code).
4. In non-interactive environments use --yes or TREEING_YES=1, not --confirm.
cli_help_topic_confirm:
Confirmation policy before writing to disk (matches the GUI "Confirm generation").

--confirm: prints paths and node count to stderr, interactive [y/N] (default No). TTY only.
--yes / -y: no prompt, treated as confirmed (explicit allow for CI / agents); prints an [INFO] audit line to stdout (omitted with --quiet).
TREEING_YES=1: same as --yes (for containers / scheduled tasks; accepts 1/true/yes/on).

Notes:
1. Default is no prompt, for script compatibility.
2. --confirm and --yes are mutually exclusive; --confirm and --json are mutually exclusive.
3. --dry-run writes nothing; if combined with --confirm, prints one [WARN] line to stdout and ignores it.
4. When the user picks N, the exit code is 0 (cancelled).

Recommended:
  --dry-run --format tree  # preview structure first
  --confirm                # manual confirm
  --yes --fail-on-conflict # unattended script
cli_help_topic_dry_run:
Run parsing and path registration, but skip mkdir/touch.
Notes:
1. With --fail-on-conflict, conflicts only warn instead of aborting.
2. Recommended before every real run.
cli_help_topic_format:
text: print an [OK]/[DRY-RUN] summary to stdout.
tree: print an ASCII preview tree to stdout (node names match the GUI preview).
Notes:
1. With --format tree, the text summary is not printed.
2. With --json, the tree_preview field is an array of strings.
cli_help_topic_input:
Read an ASCII directory tree from a file (-i) or standard input (-p).
Notes:
1. -i and -p are mutually exclusive.
2. Without -i/-p, prints --help.
3. In pipe scenarios, prefer -i or explicit -p to avoid confusion with the help subcommand.
cli_help_topic_json:
Print a single-line JSON object to stdout with ok, node_count, warnings, exit_code, etc.
Notes:
1. With --quiet, stdout contains only the JSON.
2. With --warn-exit-code, exit_code may be 2.
3. On failure, an error field is included.
cli_help_topic_output:
Specify the root directory for generated output (-o).
Notes:
1. Defaults to '.' (current working directory); in scripts, always specify it explicitly.
2. When -o is not given and the current directory is the write target, prints one [WARN] line to stdout (omitted under --dry-run; with --json --quiet it goes into a JSON field).
3. --use-settings reads last_generate_dir from ~/.treeing/settings.json (not for CI); falling back to '.' also emits a [WARN].
4. --check-writable checks write permission before writing (including not-yet-existing subpaths).
cli_help_topic_parse:
Parsing is done by build_tree; --no-fix, --strict-dirs, and --indent-unit affect the result.
Notes:
1. Parse warnings go to stdout (the [WARN] block) or --warnings-file.
2. --format tree shows the same preview tree as the GUI.
cli_help_topic_rollback_on_error:
On an exception during writing (non-dry-run), roll back files/directories created during this run.
Notes:
1. Off by default: on failure, partial results are kept.
2. Unlike --fail-on-conflict (which targets path conflicts), this targets I/O and similar errors.
3. No equivalent in the GUI.
cli_help_topic_strict:
Equivalent to --fail-on-conflict --no-fix.
Notes:
1. Suitable for strict CI runs.
2. With irregular indentation, parse warnings or virtual nodes are more likely.
cli_help_topic_windows:
(Example) Capture a tree on Windows: tree /F /A > mytree.txt
Notes:
1. For non-UTF-8 files, pass the matching codepage, e.g. --encoding cp1252.
2. The CLI already handles console encoding; PYTHONIOENCODING is usually unnecessary.
3. For scheduled tasks, set "Start in" and pass -o explicitly.
cli_help_topics_footer:
Tips: --help for a short option list | --help-full for full details | --about for about
Launch: {cli_python} | {cli_exe_unix} | {cli_exe_win}
cli_help_topics_intro:
Treeing CLI help topics.
Usage:
  {cli_python} help <topic>          # source
  {cli_exe_unix} help <topic>        # Linux / macOS executable
  {cli_exe_win} help <topic>         # Windows executable
cli_about_flag: Show about info (version, overview, licence, etc.)
cli_allow_nested_names: Treat / \ in names as path segments and create levels (trusted input only; see help allow-nested)
cli_check_writable: Extra check that the output directory is writable before generation (whether the output path is a directory is always checked)
cli_confirm: Interactive confirm [y/N] before writing (needs TTY; mutually exclusive with --yes)
cli_confirm_conflict_disabled: Without --fail-on-conflict, items with the same name may be overwritten.
cli_confirm_conflict_enabled: With --fail-on-conflict: path conflicts abort and roll back.
cli_confirm_warn_count: Parse warnings: {count} (see the stdout [WARN] block or --warnings-file).
cli_dry_run: Preview only, do not write to disk (recommended for first use)
cli_dry_run_msg: [DRY-RUN] Preview mode: {count} valid node(s), nothing created
cli_empty_tree: [ERR] Parse result is empty; nothing generated
cli_encoding: Input encoding (applies to both -i and -p; default utf-8)
cli_error_confirm_not_tty: [ERR] --confirm requires an interactive terminal; use --yes, TREEING_YES=1, or --dry-run instead
cli_fail_on_conflict: Abort and roll back on duplicate paths or type conflicts (same as GUI)
cli_format: Output format: text (default summary) or tree (ASCII preview tree, same semantics as the GUI preview)
cli_indent_unit: Indent unit in characters N (default: auto-detect; see help indent-unit)
cli_input_file: Input file path (ASCII tree text). Mutually exclusive with -p
cli_json: Output the result as JSON to stdout (for scripts and agents)
cli_no_fix: Disable indentation auto-repair (same as GUI "Disable auto-repair")
cli_no_warn_limit: Show all warnings in the terminal without truncation
cli_output_dir: Output root directory (default .; in scripts, specify it explicitly)
cli_output_is_file: [ERR] Output path is a file, not a directory: {path}
cli_paste: Read from standard input (mutually exclusive with -i; prompt on stderr)
cli_prompt_paste: Paste the tree structure, end with Ctrl+D (Linux/Mac) or Ctrl+Z (Windows):
cli_quiet: Suppress stdout warnings and text summary (common with --json; the implicit -o notice goes into the JSON)
cli_rollback_on_error: Roll back files/directories created this run on write failure (default: keep partial results)
cli_strict_dirs: Strict directory inference (same as GUI; branch lines default to directories)
cli_use_settings: When -o is not given, read last_generate_dir from ~/.treeing/settings.json
cli_warn_exit_code: Exit with code 2 when successful but with parse/generation warnings
cli_warn_implicit_output: [WARN] -o not specified; will write to the current directory: {path}
cli_warn_limit: Maximum number of warnings shown in the terminal (default 10)
cli_warnings_file: Write all warnings to a UTF-8 file (one per line, prefixed with [parse]/[generate])
cli_yes: Skip confirmation, treat as confirmed (CI / agent; mutually exclusive with --confirm)
core_err_path_conflict_rolled: Path conflict '{name}'; generation aborted (rolled back {rolled} item(s))
core_hint_indent_unit_check: An indent unit was specified manually, but parsing encountered indent repair or multiple root nodes. Verify the indent unit matches the input (CLI: --indent-unit; GUI: indent unit).
core_warn_disk_type_conflict: ⚠️ Path '{path}' is already a {existing_kind} in the output directory and cannot be created as a {new_kind}
core_warn_dot_root: The root node '.' means the current directory; contents will be generated directly into the target path
core_warn_duplicate_path: ⚠️ Duplicate path '{name}'; the later one will overwrite the earlier
core_warn_empty_input: Input is empty or has no valid nodes
core_warn_indent_jump: Indent jump: inserted virtual directory '<auto>' under '{parent}'
core_warn_inferred_dir: Inferred '{name}' as a directory from its children
core_warn_inferred_dir_heuristic: Inferred '{name}' as a directory from the branch prefix
core_warn_nested_rejected: ⚠️ Nested path '{name}' contains an illegal segment (.. or absolute path); flattened
core_warn_orphan: Line {line} orphan node '{name}' treated as a root
core_warn_path_too_long: ⚠️ Path too long ({length} chars); may fail: {path}
core_warn_path_type_conflict: ⚠️ Path '{path}' is already a {existing_kind} and cannot be created as a {new_kind}
core_warn_path_under_file: ⚠️ Path '{path}' is under file '{file}' and cannot be created
core_warn_skip_subtree: ⚠️ Node '{name}' and its subtree skipped (nested path conflict or unable to create an intermediate directory)
build_artifact_too_small: Build artifact too small: {path} ({size} bytes, minimum {min_size})
